userStories.title: Config & Auth
userStories.description:
Implement config store (conf package) and auth resolution for the Koda CLI.

Create:
- `src/config.ts` — read/write ~/.koda/config.json using Conf<{apiUrl,apiKey}>
- `src/utils/auth.ts` — resolveAuth(options): flag → KODA_API_KEY env → config fallback; apiUrl defaults to http://localhost:3100/api

Commands to implement:
- `koda login --api-key <key> [--api-url <url>]` — saves to config, prints 'Logged in successfully'
- `koda config show` — prints apiUrl + masked apiKey (show last 4 chars)
- `koda config set [--api-key <key>] [--api-url <url>]` — updates config
- `koda version` — prints package.json version
acceptanceCriteria: koda login --api-key mykey123 saves apiKey to ~/.koda/config.json
koda config show prints apiUrl and masked key (e.g. '***key123')
resolveAuth() prefers --api-key flag over KODA_API_KEY env over config file
koda version prints the version from package.json
Unit tests for resolveAuth covering all 3 fallback levels

routing.reasoning: Config boilerplate with straightforward fallback logic (flag → env → file)

userStories.title: Output Utilities
userStories.description:
Create shared output utilities used by all commands.

Create:
- `src/utils/output.ts` — output(data, {json}) for JSON vs human output; table(headers, rows) for column-aligned chalk tables; success/error/warn helpers
- `src/utils/error.ts` — EXIT codes (0 success, 1 API error, 2 config error, 3 validation error); handleApiError(err): never
- `src/client.ts` — configure hey-api Axios client with baseUrl and Bearer token header

Exit code constants:
```typescript
export const EXIT = { SUCCESS: 0, API_ERROR: 1, CONFIG_ERROR: 2, VALIDATION_ERROR: 3 };
```
acceptanceCriteria: output(data, {json: true}) prints JSON.stringify with 2-space indent
output(data, {json: false}) prints human-readable format
table() produces column-aligned output with chalk-colored headers
handleApiError() exits with code 1 for API errors, prints error message to stderr
Unit tests for output and error utilities

userStories.title: Project Commands
userStories.description:
Implement project subcommands using the generated API client.

Commands:
- `koda project list [--json]` — list all projects; table cols: Name, Key, Slug
- `koda project show <slug> [--json]` — show project details

Use ProjectsService from src/generated/. Configure client via configureClient() before each call.
acceptanceCriteria: koda project list displays Name, Key, Slug columns
koda project list --json outputs valid JSON array
koda project show <slug> displays project details
koda project show <slug> --json outputs valid JSON object
Exits with code 2 if no API key configured
Unit tests mocking ProjectsService

userStories.title: Ticket Commands
userStories.description:
Implement all ticket subcommands. Commands: - `koda ticket create --project --type bug|enhancement --title "..." [--desc "..."] [--priority low|medium|high|critical] [--json]` - `koda ticket list --project [--status ] [--type ] [--priority

] [--assigned-to ] [--unassigned] [--limit 20] [--page 1] [--json]` - `koda ticket mine [--project ] [--status ] [--json]` — shorthand for list --assigned-to self - `koda ticket show [--json]` — ref = KODA-42 (PROJECT_KEY-number) or CUID - `koda ticket verify --comment "..."` - `koda ticket assign [--to ]` — omit --to = self-assign - `koda ticket start ` - `koda ticket fix --comment "..." [--git-ref "v1.0:src/auth.ts:42"]` - `koda ticket verify-fix --comment "..." [--pass|--fail]` - `koda ticket close ` - `koda ticket reject --comment "..."` ticket list display: ``` # Type Priority Status Assignee Title KODA-42 🐛 HIGH VERIFIED subrina-coder Null ref in auth service ``` ticket show display: ``` KODA-42 — Null ref in auth service Type: BUG | Priority: HIGH | Status: VERIFIED Assignee: subrina-coder | Created by: william Description: NullPointerException in AuthService... Comments (2): [VERIFICATION] subrina-coder — 2026-03-17 Confirmed: null ref at src/auth/auth.service.ts:87 ```
acceptanceCriteria: koda ticket create --project koda --type bug --title "Test" creates a ticket and prints ref (KODA-N)
koda ticket list --project koda --json returns parseable JSON array
koda ticket mine returns tickets where assignedToAgent matches current API key
koda ticket show KODA-1 displays full ticket details with comments
koda ticket verify KODA-1 --comment "Confirmed" sends verification and prints success
koda ticket assign KODA-1 --to subrina-coder assigns the ticket
koda ticket start KODA-1 transitions ticket to IN_PROGRESS
koda ticket fix KODA-1 --comment "Fixed" sends fix report
koda ticket verify-fix KODA-1 --comment "Pass" --pass closes the fix
koda ticket close KODA-1 closes the ticket
koda ticket reject KODA-1 --comment "Reason" rejects the ticket
All commands support --json flag
Ticket ref KODA-42 resolves correctly (project key + number)
Unit tests for ticket command handlers mocking TicketsService

userStories.title: Comment & Agent Commands
userStories.description:
Implement comment add and agent info commands.

Commands:
- `koda comment add <ref> --body "..." [--type verification|fix_report|review|general]` — add comment to ticket
- `koda agent me [--json]` — show current agent info (from GET /agents/me using API key auth)
acceptanceCriteria: koda comment add KODA-1 --body "Looks good" adds a comment and prints success
koda comment add --type verification sets the CommentType correctly
koda agent me displays agent name, slug, roles, status
koda agent me --json outputs valid JSON
Unit tests for comment and agent command handlers
